legal.summary: Committee members must disclose conflicts of interest and recuse themselves from affected deliberations.

requirements.requirement: Any Committee member with a direct or indirect interest in a matter under discussion must disclose the fact and nature of the interest to the Committee
requirements.rationale: Ensures governance integrity of NCII designation and policy decisions at the highest level

requirements.requirement: Disclosed interests must be recorded in the minutes of the Committee meeting
requirements.rationale: Creates auditable record of governance compliance

technical.summary: No technical requirements for this governance provision.

governance.summary: Conflict-of-interest management framework for the national cyber security governance body.

requirements.requirement: Member must recuse from all deliberation and decision-making on the disclosed matter after making the disclosure
requirements.rationale: Prevents conflicted decision-making on NCII designations and sector determinations

legal.summary: CE directives are binding instruments that NCII entities and sector leads must comply with.

requirements.requirement: Monitor, identify and maintain a register of all CE directives issued under the Act, ensuring awareness of each directive's requirements and effective date
requirements.rationale: CE directives form a critical layer of the regulatory framework — non-compliance with any directive constitutes an offence under s18

requirements.requirement: Ensure organizational compliance with each directive within specified implementation timelines
requirements.rationale: Directives published in Gazette have binding legal force on all NCII entities

technical.summary: Technical compliance with the specific requirements of each CE directive.

requirements.requirement: Implement technical controls and configurations specified in each applicable CE directive
requirements.rationale: Directives may mandate specific technical measures such as incident reporting formats, risk assessment methodologies, or security configurations

governance.summary: Governance framework for tracking and implementing CE directives.

requirements.requirement: Establish a directive tracking and compliance monitoring process, including gap analysis for each new directive and remediation planning
requirements.rationale: Systematic approach needed given approximately 10 directives already published with more expected
